The '''Roche asteroid field''', sometimes known simply as '''Roche''', was a series of [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] colonized by the [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]], including [[Nickel One]] (the [[Capital/Legends|capital]]), [[Slayn]], [[Korpil]], [[Roche G42]], and [[Research Station Shantipole|Shantipole]].

==Description==
[[File:Roche_asteroid_belt2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The [[Out Runner]] flying through the Roche asteroid belt.]]
Some believed that the Roche asteroids were the remains of the Verpine [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], possibly destroyed by civil war. The Verpine simply claimed that they did not know where their homeworld was located. The Verpine were lead by a [[Technocracy|technocratic]] [[Verpine government|government]].<ref name="SoR" /> This government led all the Verpine worlds as a cohesive whole, though there were disparate Hive-minded governments of each planet or system.

==History==
Roche asteroids joined the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] early in its history, sometime between [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]] and [[22,000 BBY/Legends|22,000 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" />

In [[3996 BBY]], a Verpine pirate named [[Raxsus Nuli]] tried to conquer a planet, but was arrested by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. When the Wookiees joined the galactic community, they traded with the Verpine for manufacturing processes.

The asteroids were the headquarters of [[Roche Hive Mechanical Apparatus Design and Construction Activity for Those Who Need the Hive's Machines/Legends|Roche Industries]] and [[Slayn & Korpil/Legends|Slayn & Korpil]]. They were the only place where [[larmalstone]] was available. Roche also exported [[Verpine shatter gun]]s prior to the [[Jedi Civil War]].

The asteroids didn't secede during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="TEA" />

[[File:Roche_asteroid_belt.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A Roche asteroid, site of the [[Shantipole Project]].]]

After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the asteroids were the site of the [[Shantipole Project]], in which Slayn & Korpil worked with [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] to develop the [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]]. When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] became aware of this, Governor [[Bane Nothos]] [[Blockade of the Roche system|blockaded]] the system. However, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] [[Battle of Roche (Galactic Civil War)|broke]] the blockade with the B-wing prototypes and blew up [[Research Station Shantipole]],<ref name="SFS">''[[Strike Force: Shantipole]]''</ref> resulting in Nothos's demotion to Commander.<ref name="Otherspace">[[Otherspace (adventure)|''Otherspace'']]</ref>

The Verpine later joined the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], a conflict broke out between the Verpine and the [[Barabel/Legends|Barabels]]; the Barabels sold dead Verpine to the [[Kubaz/Legends|Kubaz]] as food. This dispute was eventually settled by [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]].

In [[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]], during the [[Swarm War]], the [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]] tried to take over the Roche asteroids and other insectoid-inhabited worlds of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]]. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] repulsed the invaders and saved the Verpine hive queen.<ref name="DNIII" />

In the years leading to the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], Roche was involved in a significant commercial dispute with the planet [[Murkhana/Legends|Murkhana]]. The Verpine accused Murkhana of counterfeiting Verpine technology. To solve the problem, Roche agreed to a trade agreement in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] with the powerful [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] for protection services in exchange for technological enhancements. When the war erupted between the Galactic Alliance and the [[Confederation]], Roche remained neutral.<ref name="Sacrifice">''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''</ref> However, the system was [[Battle of Roche|invaded]] by the [[Imperial Remnant]] shortly after, but the Mandalorians honored their agreement and came to the Verpine's aid.<ref name="Invincible">''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]''</ref>

To prevent future attacks on their colonies, the Verpine created the [[IX-6 heavy combat droid]]s to supplement their defenses. Sometime after the establishment of [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] in [[130 ABY]], a fleet of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] blasted several heavily populated Verpine asteroids to dust for the species' unwillingness to stop the production of the IX-6 model.<ref name="LECG">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]''</ref>
